summary: Sprint A — Mestari standalone + Voita stripped flow fully validated. Backend pytest 9/9 PASS (test_sprint_a_mestari_voita.py). (a) POST /api/voita/lead: source='mestari' → optin_consents row with surface='mestari_landing' + consent_tag='mestari_lead'; source missing/empty/invalid all fall back to surface='voita_landing' + consent_tag='voita_lead'. (b) POST /api/voita/raffles/{slug}/enter accepts and persists new optional fields: confidence (1..5), contact_channel ('telegram'|'email'), pending_id (≤64 chars); confidence=99 → 422; pending_id length>64 → 422; contact_channel='whatsapp' normalised to None server-side. Frontend e2e: /mestari mounts intro → start CTA → 5 quiz Qs (each option auto-advances to zinger which auto-advances at 2s) → mestari-tease renders profile name 'THE QUIET SHARP' → tease-continue → mestari-gate → email+rules → submit → mestari-confirm. /voita/kups-hjk-veikkausliiga-final-2026 and /voita/tappara-karpat-liiga-final-2026: NEW 8-beat flow intro→scout→pick→score→confidence→review→gate→confirm. CRITICAL QUIZ-ABSENCE CHECK: scanned DOM for [data-testid^=mestari-q-], [data-testid^=mestari-zinger-], [data-testid=mestari-tease] on /voita/{slug} → ALL ABSENT (no quiz contamination). Contact-gate Telegram CTA is rgb(34,158,217)=#229ED9 with href='https://t.me/Putkihq_bot?start={pending_id}' where pending_id is a real UUID. Email fallback (input, displayname, age, rules, submit) all present + functional. Telegram click → confirm-step renders confirm-telegram-reopen anchor with the same deep-link. Email submit (channel=email, confidence=3) → confirm-step shows 'You're in / Result lands in your inbox after kickoff' copy and HIDES confirm-telegram-reopen. Mongo verification: voita_entries row persisted with contact_channel='email', confidence=3, pending_id='2e15e712-…' and a separate telegram entry from backend test with contact_channel='telegram', confidence=5. BackToHome (data-testid=back-to-home) present top-left on both /mestari and /voita/{slug}. No console errors. Iter34 pre-existing fixture-leak failures in test_sprint_voita_hero.py intentionally not re-run per main-agent note.
test_credentials: X-Admin-Token: putki-hq-admin (admin-only endpoint used: PUT /api/admin/settings to flip voita_feature_enabled=true). Public funnel needs no auth.
critical_code_review_comments: Telegram path commit is fire-and-forget: handleTelegram() in VoitaRaffle.jsx calls submitEntry('telegram') without await before advance('confirm'). If the user closes the tab fast enough OR the network drops, the entry never reaches the backend yet the FE shows 'Entry locked'. Acceptable for Slice 3 (bot doesn't exist yet) but before launch, advance() should be gated on a successful 2xx (or at minimum the request should be queued in sessionStorage for retry).

Mestari source='mittari' is silently accepted by _PUTKI_LEAD_SOURCES and routed to mittari_landing/mittari_lead — fine — but no UI surface emits source='mittari' yet. Either remove from the whitelist or wire up the Mittari capture (forward-compatible, low risk).

VoitaRaffle.jsx is now ~790 lines with 8 step components inline. Consider extracting Intro/ScoutReport/PickStep/ScoreStep/ConfidenceStep/ReviewStep/ContactGate/Confirmation into /components/voita/ — current file is borderline maintainable.

_VoitaEntryPayload.contact_channel uses max_length=16 + manual whitelist {telegram,email} in the endpoint; cleaner as Literal['telegram','email'] | None so pydantic itself rejects invalid values with 422 (today an invalid value silently falls through as None).

The pending_id is generated client-side via crypto.randomUUID — fine — but no server-side uniqueness constraint exists. If a bot operator on Slice 3 receives `/start <pending_id>` they must accept that two entries could (in theory) share an ID. Recommend adding a unique-sparse index on voita_entries.pending_id.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_sprint_a_mestari_voita.py (new — 9 tests; voita lead source variants + entry new fields)
seed_data_creation: TEST_iter35_* prefixed emails inserted into optin_consents (4 leads) and voita_entries (3-4 entries, kups + tappara raffles). All easily filtered/cleaned by prefix. voita_feature_enabled was flipped ON to allow /enter to succeed; left ON (was already required by FE flows).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint A complete: /mestari is a standalone product (intro→5Q→5zingers→tease→email-gate→confirm, posts /api/voita/lead with source='mestari' → consent_tag='mestari_lead' / surface='mestari_landing'). /voita/{slug} has been fully stripped of quiz/zinger/tease — new flow is intro→scout→pick→score→confidence→review→gate→confirm. Contact gate Telegram CTA (#229ED9, data-testid=contact-gate-telegram) is the primary path with deep-link t.me/Putkihq_bot?start={pending_id}; email is the fallback under a divider. /api/voita/raffles/{slug}/enter persists new fields confidence/contact_channel/pending_id. Telegram bot (Slice 3) is not yet built — the deep-link is a stub; don't test bot-side resolution. Test slugs: kups-hjk-veikkausliiga-final-2026 and tappara-karpat-liiga-final-2026 (both require voita_feature_enabled=true; flip via PUT /api/admin/settings). To rerun: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_sprint_a_mestari_voita.py -v.
rca of the issue: No defects found. All Sprint A acceptance criteria met.
